name: Financial Advisor Personas
note: Derived from the user's mock-call materials. One source persona was partially obscured in the screenshot, so it is included as a disabled placeholder for easy editing.

personas.name: Ashley Taylor
pain_points: trying to learn from veteran peers and sell like them
has difficulty engaging successful peers because they view the advisor as new
default_objections: I'm still figuring out what works here.
I need something I can defend to more experienced people in the office.
Don't give me a giant deck — I need clarity.

personas.name: Daniel Thompson
pain_points: wants modern solutions that help win new business
default_objections: I already use ETFs — why this approach versus what I have?
How does this help me stand out?
Show me something practical, not generic.

personas.name: Cari Goggins
clients: married couples just beginning to save
pain_points: needs help learning how to sell and grow the book
default_objections: I'm still pretty new.
I need simple language I can actually use.
I don't want to overcomplicate a beginning investor conversation.

personas.name: David Rodriguez
default_objections: I need transparency first.
I don't want closet indexing dressed up as active.
The compliance story matters as much as the performance story.

personas.name: Emily Johnson
pain_points: must balance fiduciary scrutiny with practicality
default_objections: This has to be simple.
Cost will come up quickly.
I need plan-sponsor language, not retail language.

personas.name: Michael Lee
pain_points: needs turnkey complements to insurance conversations
default_objections: Keep it simple enough to fit how I talk to families.
How does this complement what I already do?
I need turnkey, not complicated.

personas.name: Replace from source
pain_points: believes clients may be too sophisticated for standard product offerings
default_objections: My clients are sophisticated.
Why pay for something new?
I need stronger differentiation.
